catalog.sourceHint: Parameters and prices aggregated from the official OpenAI/Anthropic pages (LiteLLM format).

combobox.validateTitle: Confirm with the provider API that the model exists
combobox.outsideCatalog: Model not in the catalog — validate it before using.

assistant.title: Assistant Settings
assistant.intro: Keys are sent only with the request and are not stored on the server. New models can be typed in and validated against the provider API (the Catalog tab shows parameters and prices).
assistant.triageSaveFailed: failed to save to the project
assistant.triagePolicyHint: The triage LLM policy is per project — written to the profile when selected.
assistant.triagePolicySelectProject: The triage LLM policy is per project. Select a project in the sidebar to configure it.
assistant.autoTitle: Generate session title via LLM (in the background)
assistant.autoTitleHint: If disabled, the title will be the first message of the chat.

apiAccess.title: API Access
apiAccess.saved: AtlasFile API key saved in this browser.
apiAccess.description: Only required when the backend runs with <c>API_AUTH_ENABLED=true</c>. The key stays in this browser only (localStorage) and is sent as <c>Authorization: Bearer</c>.
apiAccess.howtoIntro: Authentication is a deployment decision — for security, it cannot be turned on from this UI (an interface without auth must not be able to enable auth). The simplest path is to re-run the installer on the server, which generates the key, configures the <c>.env</c> and rebuilds the API while preserving your data:
apiAccess.howtoManual: Or manually: keys in <c>config/api_keys.json</c>, <c>API_AUTH_ENABLED=true</c> in .env and <c>docker compose up -d --build api mcp</c>. At the end, the generated key is shown in the terminal — paste it above in every browser that accesses AtlasFile.

language.title: Language
language.label: Interface language
language.description: Interface language. The page reloads when switching to apply it across all screens.

channels.title: Communication channels
channels.intro: Connect the assistant to external messaging channels. Channels are optional and do not affect the web chat.
channels.botHintAfter: , copy the token and paste it above.
channels.mirrorHint: When enabled, replies sent from the web chat in sessions that originated on Telegram are also forwarded to Telegram.
